title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Reddened Corners In Mouth
intro: Herbal teas for Reddened corners in mouth are a type of remedy that helps to soothe and treat angular cheilitis, a condition characterized by the reddening and inflammation of the corners of the mouth. This condition can be caused by dry mouth, vitamin deficiencies, or other factors, and herbal teas can provide relief due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Examples of herbal teas that can treat this ailment include peppermint tea, which cools and calms the area, chamomile tea, which reduces inflammation and promotes relaxation, and echinacea tea, which boosts the immune system and fights off infections. Additionally, other herbal teas such as calendula tea, sage tea, and licorice root tea can also be used to treat angular cheilitis due to their soothing and antiseptic properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat the reddened corners in the mouth, commonly known as angular cheilitis or perleche, due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including alkylamides, glycoproteins, and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation, combat infection, and promote wound healing in the affected area. Drinking Echinacea purpurea teas may help to alleviate symptoms such as redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with angular cheilitis, while also providing antioxidant benefits to the body. The antifungal properties of Echinacea purpurea teas may also help to prevent the growth of Candida albicans, a common fungus that contributes to the development of angular cheilitis.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers. You can buy it at a health store or online.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Add the 1 tablespoon of Echinacea purpurea to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it warm to help reduce redness in your mouth.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues such as nausea and stomach upset, and an increased heart rate in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat this ailment include being cautious with interactions with medications, such as blood thinners, and avoiding use in individuals with autoimmune disorders or allergies to plants in the daisy family.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea, 3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Drink the tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable improvement.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen after usage.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, also known as angular cheilitis. This herbal preparation exhibits anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness and inflammation in the affected area. The bioactive constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, in this herbal preparation possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its therapeutic effects in treating angular cheilitis. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat this ailment include reducing redness and inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from discomfort and pain.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Cut the roots into small pieces and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 2 cups of water and pour it over the roots.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe reddened corners in the mouth.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of International Society of Preventive & Community Dentistry", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may be beneficial for managing reddened corners in the mouth due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties that help prevent plaque and calculus formation and reduce gingival and periodontal diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment known as angular cheilitis, but possible side effects may include high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and changes in blood sugar levels. To minimize these risks, it is essential to consume Glycyrrhiza glabra teas in moderation, limit the duration of use to a few weeks, and be aware of individual sensitivity to its active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin.
preparation_usage: Rinse mouth with water to clear it before tea consumption.
Drink 1/2 cup of warm Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Hold tea in mouth for 2-3 minutes to let it act on red areas.
Spit out tea and rinse mouth again with water to remove residue.
Repeat this process for 7-10 days to see desired results.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat the reddened corners in the mouth ailment, also known as angular cheilitis. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm the inflamed area, reducing the redness and discomfort associated with this condition. The bioactive constituents present in Eucalyptus globulus teas, including flavonoids and terpenoids, exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which aid in preventing the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause angular cheilitis. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat angular cheilitis include rapid relief from symptoms, prevention of infection, and promotion of overall oral health.

Measure 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) for sweetness.
Place the dried Eucalyptus leaves in the tea infuser.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then remove the leaves from the infuser.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, adding honey if desired, to help soothe reddened corners in the mouth.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be beneficial in treating the reddened corners in mouth ailment, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or hives, and an increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Eucalyptus globulus teas in moderation, monitor your body's response, and avoid using it if you have a history of allergies or bleeding disorders.
preparation_usage: Rinse mouth with warm salt water to clean the area.
Steep 1 teaspoon of Eucalyptus globulus tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Gargle with the tea solution for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Repeat 2-3 times a day, as needed, for up to 7 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, commonly known as angular cheilitis. preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat the reddened corners in mouth, also known as angular cheilitis or perleche. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm the inflamed area, reducing redness and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which aid in the treatment of angular cheilitis. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat angular cheilitis include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and a decrease in the risk of infection.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1 minute.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser and put it in the pot.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser and discard the leaves.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, but its use may lead to potential side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its blood-thinning properties. To take precautions, users should start with a low dose, monitor their body's response, and avoid consuming ginkgo biloba teas with blood-thinning medications or if they have a history of bleeding disorders.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Swish the tea in your mouth for 2-3 minutes to help reduce redness.
Spit out the tea and do not swallow it.
Repeat the process 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks to see results.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat canker sores, or the reddened corners in mouth ailment. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce pain, inflammation, and prevent infection, making it an effective remedy for this condition. The bioactive constituents, including linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas has been found to provide relief from canker sores, reducing their duration and severity, and promoting overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the flowers and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop.
Pour the boiling water over the Lavandula angustifolia flowers in the cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink the tea as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, however, it may cause allergic reactions or interactions with certain medications, particularly those for diabetes and blood thinners, which can lead to prolonged bleeding or low blood sugar levels. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it's recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as well as when taking medications that affect blood clotting or blood sugar levels.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas have been traditionally used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, also known as angular cheilitis. This herbal preparation helps to treat the ailment by reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and preventing further irritation due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Sambucus nigra, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of harmful microorganisms and reducing oxidative stress. The benefits of Sambucus nigra teas in treating angular cheilitis include quick recovery, reduced pain and discomfort, and prevention of recurrence, making it a popular natural remedy among individuals seeking alternative treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Sambucus nigra flowers, leaves, and stems.
Combine 1 cup of Sambucus nigra with 4 cups of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to the tea if desired, and let it cool.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for reddened corners in the mouth.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment known as angular cheilitis, but using this herbal preparation may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications. To use Sambucus nigra teas safely, take precautions like starting with small doses, monitoring your body's response, avoiding it if you have allergies or sensitive stomach, and being cautious when combining it with other herbs or supplements.

preparations.herb_desc: Symphytum officinale teas, also known as comfrey teas, have been traditionally used to treat the reddened corners in the mouth ailment, commonly known as angular cheilitis. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce redness, swelling, and bacterial infections associated with this condition. The bioactive constituents, including allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and polyphenols, in Symphytum officinale teas have been shown to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it an effective treatment option. By consuming comfrey teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to soothe and heal the affected area, reducing discomfort and promoting overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers.
Dry the leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Symphytum officinale and add it to a cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe reddened corners in the mouth.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Chemistry & biodiversity, Symphytum officinale teas for reddened corners in mouth, which is a symptom often associated with mouth ulcers or canker sores, may provide relief due to the wound-healing properties of the plant, as scientifically demonstrated in studies published between 2000 and 2022.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Symphytum officinale teas can be used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, such as nausea or diarrhea, and allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, especially in individuals with sensitive stomachs or allergies. To use Symphytum officinale teas safely, individuals should start with a small dose, monitor their body's response, and be cautious when consuming it for extended periods, as prolonged use may lead to interactions with other medications or worsen underlying health conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas have been widely used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, also known as angular cheilitis. This herbal preparation helps to treat the ailment by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and preventing the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause the condition. The bioactive constituents of aloe vera teas, such as aloe-emodin, aloin, and vitamin E, have potent anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the affected area. Regular consumption of aloe vera teas can help to reduce symptoms of angular cheilitis, promote oral health, and prevent the recurrence of the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water and 1/4 cup of aloe vera gel. You can buy aloe vera gel at a store.
Cut 1 tablespoon of fresh aloe vera leaf and squeeze the gel into a bowl.
Mix the aloe vera gel with 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes. Do not boil.
Strain the mixture into a cup. Let it cool before drinking. Drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Biomolecules, Aloe vera teas for reddened corners in mouth may be beneficial due to the potential anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe vera, which could help alleviate oral mucosal lesions.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat the reddened corners in mouth, also known as angular cheilitis, but some people may experience side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, or an increased heart rate due to the high levels of anthraquinones present in the tea. To use Aloe vera teas safely, it's recommended to start with a small dose, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and monitor your body's response to prevent any adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as wild marjoram, have been traditionally used to treat the reddened corners in the mouth ailment, commonly known as angular cheilitis. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid, which exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that help to combat the fungal or bacterial infections causing the condition. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the underlying infection, Origanum vulgare teas help to alleviate the symptoms of angular cheilitis, including redness, swelling, and pain. The benefits of using Origanum vulgare teas to treat angular cheilitis include its natural and non-invasive approach, making it a suitable alternative to conventional medications for those seeking a holistic remedy.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Origanum vulgare leaves.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot.
Reduce heat and add the dried Origanum vulgare leaves to the pot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea while it's warm to soothe the reddened corners in your mouth.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Origanum vulgare teas, with a high Trolox equivalent antioxidant capacity (TEAC) of 5.87+/-0.2mM and total phenolics content of 1653.61+/-11.52 microM, may help alleviate reddened corners in the mouth due to its strong antioxidant properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat the reddened corners in mouth ailment, known as angular cheilitis, but may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. Precautions should be taken when using Origanum vulgare teas, including avoiding excessive consumption, monitoring for signs of intolerance or sensitivity, and being aware of potential interactions with other herbs or supplements.

intro_study: According to "Complementary therapies in medicine", teas for reddened corners in mouth, such as sage tea, may be effective in alleviating oral mucositis as indicated by a study showing that sage tea-thyme-peppermint hydrosol oral rinse reduced the incidence of oral mucositis.
